name: Neural Networks
description: Core concepts and components of artificial neural networks.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an activation function in a neuron?
questions.explain: Activation functions allow the network to learn complex non-linear relationships in data.

questions.question: Which component is responsible for adjusting weights during the training process?
questions.explain: Optimizers like SGD or Adam update weights to minimize the loss function.

questions.question: In a neural network, what does 'backpropagation' do?
options: Propagates errors backward to update weights
Adds more layers to the network
questions.explain: Backpropagation calculates the gradient of the loss function with respect to weights using the chain rule.

questions.question: Which function is commonly used in the output layer for multi-class classification?
questions.explain: Softmax turns logits into a probability distribution over multiple classes.

questions.question: What is the main disadvantage of using the Sigmoid activation function in deep networks?
questions.explain: Sigmoid saturates at extreme values, leading to very small gradients during backpropagation.

questions.question: What does a 'hidden layer' in a neural network perform?
options: Direct mapping of inputs to outputs
questions.explain: Hidden layers transform input features into higher-level representations.

questions.question: Which of these is a common technique to prevent overfitting in neural networks?
questions.explain: Dropout randomly deactivates neurons during training to prevent co-adaptation.

questions.question: What is the role of a 'Loss Function'?
options: To measure the error between prediction and target
To determine the number of neurons needed
To speed up the training process
questions.explain: The loss function quantifies how well the model's prediction matches the actual label.

questions.question: In the context of neural networks, what is an 'Epoch'?
options: One full pass through the entire dataset
The number of neurons in a layer
questions.explain: An epoch represents one complete cycle through the training dataset.

questions.question: What happens if the learning rate is set too high?
options: The model may overshoot the minimum
The weights will all become zero
questions.explain: A high learning rate can cause the optimizer to oscillate or diverge from the optimal solution.
